text.title: AWS CLI Containers + IaC Core Rules
text.summary: 5+ testable rules grounding the methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale. Includes the skip-this-methodology rule that the decision-tree maps to.

rule.statement: CloudFormation mutations MUST use `create-change-set` + `describe-change-set` + `execute-change-set`. Direct `update-stack` in production is FORBIDDEN. Change-set output MUST be reviewed before execution.

rationale.source: AWS CloudFormation docs; AWS post-mortem patterns

rule.rationale: Change-sets surface the actual delta CloudFormation will apply, including IAM diffs, deletions, and replacement-causing changes. `update-stack` skips this and has caused production outages.

rule.statement: Every IAM mutation script MUST be paired with a documented rollback script (or rollback command sequence in comments). IAM changes without a tested rollback are FORBIDDEN in production.

rationale.source: AWS security best practices; incident-response retros

rule.rationale: IAM changes that lock out the operator are not self-recoverable from inside the account. Pre-tested rollback is the only safe way to apply IAM changes under load.

rule.statement: ECS service updates MUST set `minimumHealthyPercent >= 50` and `maximumPercent <= 200` and use rolling deployments. `force-new-deployment` without bounds is FORBIDDEN for production services.
rule.rationale: Unbounded ECS deployments can drain all tasks before new ones become healthy, causing total outage. Bounded deployments preserve a working baseline during rollout.

rule.statement: Every CloudFormation `update-stack` or `execute-change-set` operation MUST be paired with a CloudWatch alarm or dashboard URL the operator watches during rollout. Mutations without an observability anchor are FORBIDDEN in production.
rule.rationale: Mutations without monitoring are mutations into the void. The observability anchor provides the rollback trigger when the mutation goes wrong.

rule.statement: Apply scripts to one service / cluster / stack per pass. Cross-account or cross-region mutations MUST be split. Skip when the operation is repeatable infrastructure — use aws-cfn-terraform-templates instead of CLI.
rule.rationale: Anti-conflation: cross-region mutations have inconsistent failure modes and partial-completion states that are hard to reason about.
